definition: Small perception-shaping signals (credibility, scarcity, social proof) applied before sales interaction boost conversion without changing offer architecture.
rationale: Centroid too vague; members split into distribution-lever (Demand Problem/Unlocking) vs. pre-call perception-framing (Pre-Framing) vs. membership upsell sequencing.

splits.definition: Growth bottleneck is distribution, not offer design; iterate existing channels before building new offers.
Small credibility/scarcity signals boost downstream conversion without altering the core offer.
Upsell membership after delivery of emergency/core service to leverage existing client relationship and proven pain.

definition: 10-stage progression from zero revenue to $100M+, mapped across eight business functions, with clear graduation criteria. <1% of companies complete full roadmap.
rationale: High intra-cluster cohesion (min_sim 0.932); all members reference the same 10-stage framework and completion rarity.

definition: Creator credibility increases when personal values align with audience expectations; perceived authenticity drives loyalty and repeat consumption.
rationale: Centroid conflates parasocial connection (authenticity driving loyalty) with measurement method (engagement vs. follower count) and psychology (values vs. appearance matching). Split into persona-audience alignment and influence-measurement methodology.

splits.definition: Audiences build loyalty around perceived alignment with creator values; parasocial connection increases repeat consumption and brand defense.

splits.canonical_name: Influence Measurement: Engagement Over Follower Count
splits.definition: Influence magnitude correlates with positive-interaction frequency, not absolute follower count; engagement is the operative metric.
Audiences follow creators with whom they share values/vibe or perceive physical/demographic similarity; both psychological and literal matching drive followership.

definition: Identity is the aggregate of repeated actions, not a fixed essence or past circumstance; viewing identity as behavior-changeable removes emotional friction from change.
rationale: Tight cohesion (min_sim 0.896) on core insight: actions > narrative, behavior > essence, agency > victimhood.

definition: Add qualification gates (questionnaire, wound-size scoring, budget pre-qualification) before consultation to eliminate unqualified leads and improve close efficiency.
rationale: Core cluster is tight lead-filtering. 'Lead Quality vs Lead Form Trade-offs' is a quantified-axis node (5.25% show-up rate comparison) that belongs in a measurement/benchmarking cluster, not tactic design. Kick it to preserve cohesion.
kick_members: Lead Quality vs Lead Form Trade-offs

definition: Simplify landing-page friction, dedicate pages to ad traffic, optimize sales messaging and lead-scoring backend to improve conversion funnel by 2-3x.
rationale: Cohesive (min_sim 0.929) on front-end and back-end conversion levers.

definition: Daily geographic proximity and consistent networking expand serendipity surface area, accelerating opportunity clustering and mentorship access.
rationale: Tight dyad (min_sim 0.981) with high face validity.

definition: Motivation is inverse of deprivation; individuals are driven by what they lack relative to reference point. Scarcity (desperation) drives action more than aspirational messaging.
rationale: Tight cohesion (min_sim 0.925) on core mechanism: lack/deprivation → motivation > aspirational narratives.

definition: Ongoing-share referral incentives (10-15%) outperform one-time spiffs; goodwill (value-minus-price gap) drives referral and repeat-purchase velocity.
rationale: Cohesive theme of turning customer satisfaction into leverage (referral, retention). Rename to broaden beyond 'programs' to include goodwill as underlying driver.

definition: Break abstract traits into observable micro-behaviors; train each discrete behavior via checklist/repetition rather than coaching abstract qualities.
rationale: Tight cohesion (min_sim 0.929) on core pedagogical principle: decomposition > abstraction.

definition: Identify organic content winners first; measure engagement/CTR, then scale winners via paid distribution. Content and ads are one system, not separate channels.
rationale: Cohesive (min_sim 0.945) on sequence: organic-first learning → paid leverage.

definition: Industry averages guarantee mediocrity because they measure median performers; benchmark against top-decile operators and ignore 'good enough' frames.
rationale: Rename to shift from outcome ('margin improvement') to methodology ('winner-vs-median benchmarking'). Frames the load-bearing lever clearly.

definition: Top 10% holds disproportionate wealth; target affluent customers, not broad-base or bottom 50%. Profitability scales with customer wallet concentration.
Test hundreds of content variations weekly on same themes; audiences need reminders more than novelty. Avoid burnout by committing to enjoyable format, then optimize.
rationale: Cohesive (min_sim 0.894) on iteration discipline: variation > novelty, theme consistency > daily invention.

definition: Premature scaling across locations/divisions compounds operational debt faster than slow single-market optimization. Fix unit economics before expanding.
rationale: Tight cohesion (min_sim 0.951) on cautionary principle: single-market perfection > multi-market mediocrity.
